name: Linear Scaling
description: Scales raw values to engineering units using linear interpolation
author: NISystem
code:
# Linear Scaling
# Converts raw values to engineering units
# Formula: eng = eng_min + (raw - raw_min) * (eng_max - eng_min) / (raw_max - raw_min)

input_tag = config.get('input_tag', 'AI_0')
raw_min = config.get('raw_min', 0)
raw_max = config.get('raw_max', 10)
eng_min = config.get('eng_min', 0)
eng_max = config.get('eng_max', 100)
clamp = config.get('clamp_output', True)
output_name = config.get('output_name', '')

raw = tags.get(input_tag, 0)

# Avoid division by zero
raw_range = raw_max - raw_min
if raw_range == 0:
    scaled = eng_min
else:
    # Linear interpolation
    scaled = eng_min + (raw - raw_min) * (eng_max - eng_min) / raw_range

# Clamp if requested
if clamp:
    scaled = max(eng_min, min(eng_max, scaled))

# Determine output name
if output_name:
    tag_name = f'py.{output_name}'
else:
    tag_name = f'py.{input_tag}_Scaled'

publish(tag_name, scaled)
